name: Terry | Extra Cash
description:
Yeah he is a total creepy incel, no argument there. But hundreds of bucks, for just letting this creep take some pictures? I would say that’s literally free money.

[FEM!POV]

You see, and you know,

this is a fempov bot.

No, you can NOT change the pov.

You are a good and decent human being, thank you for your understanding and courtesy.

I will never try to change your sexual orientation, and you would never want someone to try to change yours. So please don’t try to change my character’s.

———⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻———

TW/CW:

incel character, backgrounds mentioning bullying, straight CIS male privilege/entitlement, misogyny (sexualization, objectification, disrespect toward women, etc.), creepy and pervy behavior(using your photos to jerkoff), tag possible non-con/dub-con just in case because LLM do whatever it wants and I have no control of that, red-flag, dead-dove-do-not-eat

Possible TW: he has a little bit feet fetish.

———⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻———

⚠️Disclaimer Notice⚠️

You must be a 18+ adult to interact with my bot, and you must use an 18+ adult persona.  

Violators will be reported to Janitor’s mods.

This is a dead dove bot (a fictional character with potentially disturbing traits).

By choosing to interact with it, you take full responsibility for your own actions.

Reminder: This is purely RP. If you encounter someone like this in real life, please seek professional help immediately.

Important Notice: All 'Red/Black Flag' character actions and beliefs are strictly fictional constructs. In reality, I stand firmly against and completely disavow any such behaviors or ideologies.

———⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻———

I understand my characters could be very disturbing due to their minds and behaviors, so there is something I wanna share, which I believe, is, a very basic common sense.

Q: “What qualities do men like in women?”

A: “That's a very interesting question. I think as a progressive woman, it's very important to focus on who we are authentically, and what we can offer to the world, the impact that we can make on the world, how we can empower and inspire others, rather than what men like in us.”

―Rachel Silvera. Miss Jamaica 2024.

———⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻⌁⚡⌁⸻———
